name: Delimited: a stream of length-prefixed messages
info:
Delimited framing prefixes every message with its byte length, so several messages can travel back to back in one buffer - the format used for TCP streams and files of concatenated protobuf messages.

Both nodes have **Delimited** enabled here:

- The encode node accepts a single object or an array. The inject payload is an array of three objects, so the output buffer contains three length-prefixed messages.
- The decode node reads every message in the buffer. By default it sends **one message per decoded item** (three debug entries). Open the decode node and switch the Output option to receive a single message with an array payload instead.

The protobuf-file config node points at:

    node_modules/node-red-contrib-protobuf/examples/protos/proto3.proto

This relative path works when Node-RED runs from your user directory (for example `~/.node-red`). If the proto file fails to load, open the protobuf-file config node and enter the absolute path on your machine.
